title: Countess
content:
The pig's head omelette - 
        something akin to a tatoo
        buried squarely on the upper
        torso of the man
        wielding an axe,
        chopping wood.
        Shoulders drooped,
        the bizarre rendition
        had a female
        counterpart
         - a snake, fitted like a
        fish-net stocking,
        coating the upper leg of
        the dancer writhing to music,
        so soporific,
        near the copper shield
        of the table,
        ever-molten ash,
        air-borne with the foetid smear
        & puff of cigarette smoke.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Paul Cameron Brown delivers a powerful performance in "Countess"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
